body: Cross-card scope: spec touches both 0005-drive and 0006-rally because the layout migration is one substrate-wide change — splitting it would create a window where drive sidecars are flat and rally sidecars are folder, which complicates resumption and tooling. ac-06 (existing-folder policy) and ac-09 (post-ship integration test) are deferred to implementation time when the actual on-disk state can be inspected.

Engineering finding 2026-05-09: orbit's canonicaliser + verify scan .orbit/specs/*.yaml as specs. The sidecar shape <id>.drive.yaml therefore fails parse ('unknown field spec_id, expected one of id, goal, cards, status, labels, acceptance_criteria'). The migration must include teaching orbit's scanner about sidecars — either (a) skip files matching <id>.<sidecar>.yaml when the prefix is a known spec id, (b) parse them under a separate sidecar schema, or (c) place sidecars in a sub-namespace .orbit/sidecars/<id>.<sidecar>.yaml. This is the crux of the migration: not just changing paths in skill files but extending the substrate scanner. Add an AC covering this when implementing the sidecar spec.
